ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63522..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63522..remark: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63522..description: Long skirt in white silk cady. Spiral construction where wide band of doubled up fabric crisscrosses under bust, hugs hips, then creates a wrap-around effect all the way down. Top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Band type cut offset by diapason at mid front neckline. Bolero in white silk cady. Basically a shell with funnel sleeves. Thick application randomly all over of silk gazar and organza flowers in various shapes, sizes and colors. Small bias-cut ruffles plus cloth leaves & stems heighten the big bouquet sensation. Topstitched black ribbon knotted into a half bow.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63522..working_process: Hand application of silk flowers all over
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63522..materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk organza, silk gazar

ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63527..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63527..remark: An apparently simple crewneck dress finds new definition thanks to the trumpet skirt effect. It’s a master stroke. Adding to the upbeat appeal and the swingy feel are the bias cuts, the sunburst pleats, the playful prints and the small multicolor sequins.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63527..description: Dress in multicolor confetti print silk chiffon (fuchsia, red, yellow, turquoise, green, royal blue, white). Ankle-length siren style with round neck, straight padded shoulders and slim elbow sleeves. Soft chromatic degradé effect along hem. Two-part construction: an outer layer with vertical parallel cuts from shoulder to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias; an inner layer with sunburst pleats peeking out from two slits at mid front and mid back, respectively.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63527..materials: Confetti print silk chiffon (Stucchi, Como; art. 5113, des. 40549, var. 001, 100% silk), silk chiffon in polkadot print (Sisan, Milano; art. 68 G/G, des. 1049, 100% silk), sequins

ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..remark: Here the impression is of a sinuous female body blossoming from a bustier and an elementary skirt. The corolla effect of the bodice derives from a masterly manipulation of geometric figures (cones and truncated cones). The dress also has the feel of a column with capital.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..description: Dress with white silk gazaar bodice, long straight black silk cady skirt. Yellow silk ribbon belt with decorative topstitching. The bodice consists of a strapless bustier with two mostly overlapping stand-up truncated cone elements set at waist. Other circular pieces are folded over and inserted in back to create a corolla or ruffle effect
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..exhibitions: “1951-2001. Made in Italy Memoria”, Palazzo della Triennale, Milano, 2001
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63532..materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. white, 100% silk), silk cady (Stucchi, Como; art. 5115, 100% silk)

ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63541..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63541..remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63541..present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63541..description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with dark blue 4 cm wide stripes (20 cm spacing). In reality, forming this skirt are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in dark blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Top in dark blue wool/silk with black glass tube beading. Slim fit, round neck. Totally open back with crisscrossing ribbon straps. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63541..materials: Cotton piqué in horizontal stripe print (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1055), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. blue), silk organza, glass tube beads

ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63542..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63542..remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63542..description: Long skirt in silk jacquard with fine white/navy striping. Creating the voluminous effect are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the silk jacquard, the other a plain tone silk gazar – softly gathered into six groups of pleats at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in navy blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Allover black (vertical) glass tube beading.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63542..materials: White/navy stripe herringbone-weave silk jacquard (Sisan, Milano; art. 4382, col. 10, 100% silk), wool/silk blend (Taroni, Como; art. 77055, col. blue), silk gazar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. black, 100% silk), white cotton piqué

ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63543..label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63543..remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Again, it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63543..description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with navy blue striping. Creating the voluminous effect are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in black wool/silk satin. Slim line, knee length. T-shirt in wool/silk with navy blue glass tube beading. Slim fit, ultra low-cut V neck, elbow-length sleeves. While the alignment of the tube beads is mostly vertical, along the neckline and the lower edge of sleeves it is horizontal, so forming a border. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
ALTA-MODA/SS1987/technical_sheets/63543..materials: White cotton piqué with navy blue striping (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1036), silk organza, wool/silk satin (Taroni, art. 17055, col. black)

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/photocolor/.remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/5551.remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/advertising/slides/6284.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.object: Completo abito e giacchino (abito non presente in archivio)

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Dress all in silk georgette with zigzagging silk tape overprinted in a red/yellow/green/sky blue/lilac/black floral motif. Form- fitting two-piece construction: strapless top (diff. drawing); knee-length skirt. Red suede belt with rectangular buckle to match.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Silk tape, silk georgette, cotton piqué
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.working_process: Silk tape sewn onto silk georgette and then overprinted
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: True to Ferré tradition, here the simplicity is only apparent. What seems to be a plisse print fabric is actually the result of a lengthy process where silk tape is first sewn on by hand or machine and then lavishly overprinted. What seems to be no more than a skinny bustier-dress captures in back a flowers-fluttering-in-the-wind allure thanks to the rich decorative elements.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Camicia di natté di seta nero, di linea diritta che si chiude a sormonto, con collo montante. Al centro schiena un cannone di cm. 6. Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Gonna di piquet di cotone a righe bianco-rosso, composta da due pannelli uniti a formare una ruota intera la cui ampiezza è completamente riportata sul davanti con motivo a pieghe, con ampie volute. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Nattè di seta; piquet di cotone a piccoli motivi in rilievo stampato a righe h. cm. 5; crinolina di nylon; gazaar di seta; canneté di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs
Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. Chiusura posteriore con zip e bottoni automatici ricoperti.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.working_process: Applicazioni all over di fiori ricamati
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/3.remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/7.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/7.description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/7.materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/7.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_drawings/7.remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.materials: Nattè di seta; piquet di cotone a piccoli motivi in rilievo stampato a righe h. cm. 5; crinolina di nylon; gazaar di seta; canneté di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.description: Camicia di natté di seta nero, di linea diritta che si chiude a sormonto, con collo montante. Al centro schiena un cannone di cm. 6. Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Gonna di piquet di cotone a righe bianco-rosso, composta da due pannelli uniti a formare una ruota intera la cui ampiezza è completamente riportata sul davanti con motivo a pieghe, con ampie volute. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs
Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.notes: embellished dress with frill detail at shoulders

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. Chiusura posteriore con zip e bottoni automatici ricoperti.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.working_process: Applicazioni all over di fiori ricamati
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/599.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/600.materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/600.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/600.description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/600.working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/600.remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/605.materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/605.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/605.description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/605.working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/605.remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Silk tape, silk georgette, cotton piqué
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: Dress all in silk georgette with zigzagging silk tape overprinted in a red/yellow/green/sky blue/lilac/black floral motif. Form- fitting two-piece construction: strapless top (diff. drawing); knee-length skirt. Red suede belt with rectangular buckle to match.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.working_process: Silk tape sewn onto silk georgette and then overprinted
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: True to Ferré tradition, here the simplicity is only apparent. What seems to be a plisse print fabric is actually the result of a lengthy process where silk tape is first sewn on by hand or machine and then lavishly overprinted. What seems to be no more than a skinny bustier-dress captures in back a flowers-fluttering-in-the-wind allure thanks to the rich decorative elements.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.

Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Gonna di piquet di cotone a righe bianco-rosso, composta da due pannelli uniti a formare una ruota intera la cui ampiezza è completamente riportata sul davanti con motivo a pieghe, con ampie volute. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs
Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. Chiusura posteriore con zip e bottoni automatici ricoperti.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.working_process: Applicazioni all over di fiori ricamati
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.colors: black, white, red, yellow, blue, green

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/606.remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/607.materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/607.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/607.description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/607.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/fashion_show_photos/607.remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62472.j.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62472.j.description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62472.j.materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62472.j.working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62472.j.remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62473.j.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62473.j.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62473.j.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62473.j.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62473.j.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.description: Camicia di natté di seta nero, di linea diritta che si chiude a sormonto, con collo montante. Al centro schiena un cannone di cm. 6. Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Gonna di piquet di cotone a righe bianco-rosso, composta da due pannelli uniti a formare una ruota intera la cui ampiezza è completamente riportata sul davanti con motivo a pieghe, con ampie volute. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.materials: Nattè di seta; piquet di cotone a piccoli motivi in rilievo stampato a righe h. cm. 5; crinolina di nylon; gazaar di seta; canneté di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs
Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62477.j.remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62480.j.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62480.j.description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62480.j.materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62480.j.working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62480.j.remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.description: Dress all in silk georgette with zigzagging silk tape overprinted in a red/yellow/green/sky blue/lilac/black floral motif. Form- fitting two-piece construction: strapless top (diff. drawing); knee-length skirt. Red suede belt with rectangular buckle to match.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.materials: Silk tape, silk georgette, cotton piqué
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.working_process: Silk tape sewn onto silk georgette and then overprinted
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/material_sheets/62489.j.remark: True to Ferré tradition, here the simplicity is only apparent. What seems to be a plisse print fabric is actually the result of a lengthy process where silk tape is first sewn on by hand or machine and then lavishly overprinted. What seems to be no more than a skinny bustier-dress captures in back a flowers-fluttering-in-the-wind allure thanks to the rich decorative elements.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/studies/22835.jpg.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/studies/22835.jpg.description: Knee-length dress with inner structure consisting of a strapless eight-segment seven-whalebone bustier. Horizontal topstitching with 1 cm spacing, zip closure at mid back. Sewn onto the bustier is a red/yellow/green/blue/lilac/black floral print gazar minidress with silk jersey lining. The outer part consists of five circular pieces of fabric – three all in gazar, two offset by crinoline – forming a rich swirl effect in front thanks to the plissé motif. 10 cm gazar hem. Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/studies/22835.jpg.materials: Silk gazar: nylon crinoline; silk jersey; cotton cloth
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/studies/22835.jpg.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/studies/22835.jpg.remark: Sparking sensations of levity and motion, this dress has a sculptural feel with all the fullness in front. The gazar's firmness contributes to ensuring the plasticity.

Manica ampia a kimono, lunghezza al gomito, trattenuta con quattro pieghe da un polsino sbieco chiuso con un bottone ricoperto. Lunghezza primo fianco. Completamente doppiata con lo stesso tessuto. Il volume della gonna è sorretto da una crinolina con orlo di cm. 28. Piccoli bottoni e gancio in metallo chiudono la gonna sul dietro. Lunghezza alla caviglia con orlo asimmetrico,più lungo sul dietro Cintura di colore bianco e nero in canneté di seta con fibbia ricoperta.

“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; Storie di Moda. campari e lo Stile” , HQs
Campari Group, Sesto San Giovanni, 2018/2019;
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.object: Completo composto da camicia e gonna
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: Il capo evoca leggerezza e movimento e il gioco delle righe su una forma circolare crea un effetto di geometrie inaspettate

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré Couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Abito formato da due parti tra loro sovrapposte. La prima, interna e a bustier, è di linea fasciante e ha una lunghezza sopra il ginocchio. La seconda, esterna e costruita a ruota, è lunga alla caviglia e montata in sbieco. Grazie alle impunture, il tessuto tartan è assemblato a un leggero strato di ovattina, così da sostenere il volume e la corposità della ruota. E’ presente un sotto-abito in crinolina in cui sono accostati ra loro diversi segmenti circolari che seguono l’andamento del capo. Nella parte inferiore, è posizionata un’alta balza mossa da profonde pieghe, che enfatizza ulteriormente il volume.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Taffetà di seta tinto in filo a motivo tartan color rosso, giallo, verde e bluette; piquet di cotone bianco; crinolina di nylon nero; piquet di cotone bianco
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: L’anima di questo abito è più che mai sfaccettata, singolare, curiosa. In esso si sommano i colori vivissimi della Martinica, accostati però tra loro in una delle fantasie più tradizionali e antiche dell’Europa, il tartan. Non solo: se il colore pieno esprime il piacere estremo per la fantasia, la gioia, lo slancio, la precisione della struttura porta in sé tutta l’importanza della geometria, dell’architettura, della perizia sartoriale.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: The dress has two parts, an inner one and an outer one. The inner part is a bustier-dress mid thigh in length made from eight cuts of cloth. The four-whalebone back zip bustier features horizontal rows of topstitching 1 cm apart. The outside of the dress, which is sewn onto the upper part of the bustier, consists of four empty circles all in different sizes. Two circles originate halfway in back, move toward and then rotate at the front before returning to the starting point. Meanwhile, a medium size circle starts from the mid front and performs the same type of rotation in the opposite direction. Albeit sewn on simply to the bustier, too, the largest circle is free to drape, so forming voluminous spirals at various heights.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Silk gazaar: nylon crinoline; nylon tulle
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.exhibitions: “Gianfranco Ferré Designs”, Phoenix Art Museum, Phoenix, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: With this creation Gianfranco Ferré attained a unique sense of levity, as if a warm breeze were blowing and from time to time a swirl of air overcame the force of gravity. Thus the dress shifts gently forward, in a plastic movement guaranteed by the delicacy of the gazar fabric.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Jacket in red wool satin. Singlebreasted, hip skimming. Bell shape in front, trumpet cut in back. High collar with rim in black wool satin. Boxy shoulders, radial arm construction. Long sleeves with black wool satin rim. Skirt in white wool satin with vertical stripes in black velvet ribbon. Eight-part construction. Slim line, high waist, knee length. Shirt in white silk gazar. Crossover style with three-button side closure. Mannish collar, no sleeves, (upper) hip-skimming length. Double scarf in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, blue, red, green, yellow).
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Wool satin, silk gazar, cotton velvet ribbon, silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.working_process: Application in parallel stripes (with 1.5 cm spacing) of silk velvet ribbon on wool satin fabric
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: A daytime jacket vaguely reminiscent of a tailcoat. The dandyish hint of eccentricity comes through mostly in the stripe & plaid mix.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Jacket in striking white&black stripe tubular wool. Slim mid thigh-skimming line with simple one-button closure opening toward bottom. Long sleeves with radial arm construction. Straight slit neckline slanting upwards. Skirt in red tubular wool. Six-part construction. Slim line, knee length.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.exhibitions: “Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: The mannish inspiration behind this daytime jacket is offset by some distinct sartorial maneuvers: namely, the elimination of collar & lapels and the creation of a flare effect in front. The size and positioning of the stripes are clearly designed to make a style statement.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.object: Completo abito e giacchino (abito non presente in archivio)

Small snap-buttons for the closure in back.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Dress all in silk georgette with zigzagging silk tape overprinted in a red/yellow/green/sky blue/lilac/black floral motif. Form- fitting two-piece construction: strapless top (diff. drawing); knee-length skirt. Red suede belt with rectangular buckle to match.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Silk tape, silk georgette, cotton piqué
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.working_process: Silk tape sewn onto silk georgette and then overprinted
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: True to Ferré tradition, here the simplicity is only apparent. What seems to be a plisse print fabric is actually the result of a lengthy process where silk tape is first sewn on by hand or machine and then lavishly overprinted. What seems to be no more than a skinny bustier-dress captures in back a flowers-fluttering-in-the-wind allure thanks to the rich decorative elements.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Abito al ginocchio con scollo all’americana che avvolge il collo con accenni di revers e che lascia scoperta la schiena. Nella parte anteriore l’abito presenta un accenno di baschina, con effetto marsina, staccata rispetto all’abito e sostenuta dalla crinolina di nylon, che prende avvio dalla parte posteriore dei fianchi. Chiusura posteriore con zip e bottoni automatici ricoperti.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Pizzo macramé di cotone; crinolina di nylon; chiffon di seta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.working_process: Applicazioni all over di fiori ricamati
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: In un voluto gioco di contrasti si sommano la contemporaneità della silhouette con ispirazioni vagamente maschili, il rigore del nero assoluto, la preziosità del pizzo macramé e gli effetti trompe-l’oeil.

ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.description: Shirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Crossover style, no fastening. Mannish collar. Long wide sleeves with bias-cut corolla cuffs. Big open pleat from neck to mid back. Skirt in scotch plaid silk taffeta (black, red, avian blue, green, yellow). Plaid cotton doubling (red, green, yellow, black). Circular cut, knee length. Hand topstitching. Ascot in white silk gazar.
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.materials: Silk taffeta, cotton cloth, silk gazar
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.working_process: Extensive 1 cm parallel topstitching in sync with the skirt’s circular cut
ALTA-MODA/SS1988/technical_drawings/capi.remark: Here attention goes primarily to the robust fabric (thanks to both doubling and topstitching) and to the natural sense of shape and volume, very much a matter of cut.

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.materials: Silk marocain with panel type print (Ratti, Como; art. 0457, des. F69413, var. 41), black silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.description: Jacket in 7/8 length with straight line, round neck. Rich puff sleeves with silk organza layer on the inside. Skirt in silk cady. Straight line with narrow waistband. Floor length, back slit. Top debardeur style in silk cady. Blousy effect, hip-hugging basque. Belt in Vienna straw, with matching rectangular leather buckle.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.remark: While stopping well above the knee, this jacket captures all the flair of the magnificent mid 19th century evening cloaks (1830s to ‘50s) typically complementing full-length body-skimming fourreaux. What both the original versions and Ferré’s revisitation have in common are two primary features: firstly, the gorgeous fabric (here a fabulous silk marocain) that gives structure and emphasis to a design basically simple of construction; and secondly, the lavish beauty of the soft puff sleeves. Equally important in creating the sense of richness is the intentional decorative complexity and utter sophistication of the print motif, namely Vienna-strawlike rosettes making a stunning impression on a black background and then intensifying of intricacy on the sleeves (in a twofold manner: plain tone for one part, paisley pattern for the other).
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.exhibitions: “Altre emozioni”, Palazzo Pitti, Firenze, 2000; “Moda tra analogie e dissonanze”, Firenze, 2010
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.materials: Silk gros grain (Redaelli, Milano; art. Super Ivonne), lace (Ricamificio Zibetti, Gallarate; art. 1156), nylon (“Moda e Guarnizioni” - art. 53), silk organza (Taroni, Como), silk crepe
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.description: Evening dress in three pieces: Bodice in black silk gros grain. Construction in seven small panels diminishing in size from center outward. Whalebone at seams. Topstitched black faille waistband. Back zip closure. Flounce skirt open in front. In ecru color tulle. Doubling in ivory lace with floral embroidery. Faux-raffia nylon ruffles. Ecru silk organza lining. Underskirt in ecru silk crepe with doubling in lace (same as skirt). Knee length with semi scallop-edge hem. Side zip closure. Organza rose decor.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.remark: Despite the simple, straightforward image of this contemporary low-cut top, the construction process is definitely complex. Whatever, all the visual, material & decorative opulence is in the bottom part of the dress, both in the short slim (albeit ultra precious) underskirt and in the sumptuous flounce skirt so wonderfully reminiscent of 19th century petticoats. Complete with rich embroidery & ruffles, the primary skirt finds a further note of novelty in the reinvention of nylon mesh in the form of woven Vienna straw. Donation to the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (2000)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.bibliography: “Gianfranco Ferré. I quaderni di Pitti 01”, Giunti Editore, Firenze, 2000
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/magazines_s.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/photocolors.materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 16840, col. 94), organza flowers/twigs and raffia spikelets (“Moda e Guarnizioni” art. 9808 - 126 col. gold)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/photocolors.description: Two-piece evening dress in sandy beige silk taffeta. Slim whalebone bodice with wide band along strapless neckline all about embroidery and other lavish ornamentation (gold sequins, gold metallic mesh, gold beads, organza flowers, raffia spikelets). Back zip closure. Long straight skirt with back slit. Bustier-belt in tulle, raffia and gold metallic mesh with rich embroidery & beading (fine gold silk cord, gold and wood beads). Wide swingy basque, spiral edging. Back snap closure. Shawl accessory.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/photocolors.remark: An intentional trompe l’oeil effect defines the heart & soul of this evening dress. The bodice is actually a wide bustier-belt mimicking an exquisite piece of sculpture or a supremely feminine type of armor with beautifully swirling basque. This fabulous creation – complete with amazing floral decoration – complements an airy light and absolutely fluid silk taffeta skirt. This is the second prototype. The original runway one is on display at the Pitti Palace Costume Gallery in Florence (donation in 2000).
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/photocolors.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.materials: Silk marocain with panel type print (Ratti, Como; art. 0457, des. F69413, var. 41), black silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.description: Jacket in 7/8 length with straight line, round neck. Rich puff sleeves with silk organza layer on the inside. Skirt in silk cady. Straight line with narrow waistband. Floor length, back slit. Top debardeur style in silk cady. Blousy effect, hip-hugging basque. Belt in Vienna straw, with matching rectangular leather buckle.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.remark: While stopping well above the knee, this jacket captures all the flair of the magnificent mid 19th century evening cloaks (1830s to ‘50s) typically complementing full-length body-skimming fourreaux. What both the original versions and Ferré’s revisitation have in common are two primary features: firstly, the gorgeous fabric (here a fabulous silk marocain) that gives structure and emphasis to a design basically simple of construction; and secondly, the lavish beauty of the soft puff sleeves. Equally important in creating the sense of richness is the intentional decorative complexity and utter sophistication of the print motif, namely Vienna-strawlike rosettes making a stunning impression on a black background and then intensifying of intricacy on the sleeves (in a twofold manner: plain tone for one part, paisley pattern for the other).
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.materials: Silk lace in lovely bois de rose hue, taffeta with sweet bouquet design, garland & flower decors (Ottolenghi, Milano), bois de rose silk tulle (Zecchini, Milano), black nylon tulle
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.description: Tunic A-line style. Scoop neckline, 7/8 length. Construction featuring the hand application of lace, three layers of (different) fabric sewn together with 1 cm parallel topstitching. Skirt with straight line, no waistband. Same type of construction as for tunic. Hat in natural straw. Large size, dome style. Hand sewn. Flowers in silk organza.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.remark: While lines are essentially simple – as in gently flaring tunic and straight knee-skimming skirt – this outfit succeeds in capturing all the aristo allure of once-upon-a-time lingerie, elegantly sensual, exquisitely seductive though not openly sexy. What works the special magic is the delicate bois de rose hue. Yet most of all the trick is in the airy lightness of the fundamental material, the tulle that is taken to the next level on the fascination front thanks to the gorgeous lace appliquéwork, the fine horizontal topstitching, the garland & flower ornamentation, the sublime layering with taffeta.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.working_process: Lace in appliqué form; parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.description: Dress in layers of silk georgette. Empire style, long trumpet skirt. Torchon crossover band at bustline setting in motion deep unpressed pleat all the way down to asymmetric hem: ankle skimming in front, with small train in back. Bolero with elaborate gold thread openwork/spiral embroidery in front. Perfectly symmetric lapels. Semi sheer mid back thanks to layers of tulle & mesh topstitched together with a delicate gold thread. At back neck and base of bolero two pairs of ringlets. Small silk flowers on shoulders, short sleeves and part of front.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.remark: An exquisitely simple black evening dress with asymmetric flaring toward bottom finds an element of distinction in the torchon effect that gives definition to an empire line. By contrast, the sumptuous little jacket bordering on a bolero sparks images of a precious English Tudor or Spanish jerkin. It’s all about the spirals reminiscent of the famous Thonet Style (wood chairs) that become all the more luxurious thanks to the chunky flower decors, inserts & ringlets in a variety of materials. Nicely offsetting this quasi-baroque impact is the transparency in back.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.working_process: Hand application of silk flowers; spiral thread embroidery/topstitching (by hand) on tulle, nylon and lurex, so joining the three fabrics in layers; hand embroidery in raffia, complete with relief effect; hand embroidery (in openwork pattern) of gold lurex spiral thread.

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.exhibitions:
“Ferré. Abito come accento che esce dal corpo”, area Bernardelli, Mantova, 2015; “Gianfranco Ferré. Moda, un racconto nei
disegni”, Centro Culturale Santa Maria della Pietà, Cremona, 2017;
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.materials: Black silk georgette (Clerici, Como; art. 1031, col. 9), beige silk organza (Clerici, Como), raffia, gold lurex diagonal mesh, stiff white nylon tulle, gold lurex spiral thread, iridescent beige silk taffeta
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/5658.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.object:
Abito da sera
NON PRESENTE IN ARCHIVIO/Donazione galleria Costume Firenze
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.materials: Organza di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 1540/203); tulle di seta (Zecchini, Milano).
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.description: Gonna in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle e in organza di seta in tinta che avvolge la vita come una cintura; chiusura posteriore con automatici. Sul fianco sinistro sono inseriti due pannelli in pizzo color crema doppiato in tulle, sovrapposti e di dimensioni diverse, con orlo festonato, ripresi, a formare doppio piegone con doppia balza di tulle plissettato alla cintura e organza di seta color rosa e color carne. Sul fianco sinistro, applicazione di rose e ramoscelli in organza e rafia naturale, color grigio perla e rosa cipria. Fodera in organza di seta a righe rosa e color carne. Body in tulle elasticizzato color carne, ricamato con perline in varie tonalità di rosa e argento, foderato in crepe di seta in tinta. Chiusura posteriore con automatici.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.remark: La composta asimmetria dei volumi, la cui ricchezza è intenzionalmente tutta spostata lateralmente, il contrasto tra lunghezze, più che misurate nella gonna in sé e decisamente importanti nei pannelli ad inserto, diversi per dimensioni e “raccordati” alla gonna stessa da un “bouquet” floreale multimaterico, segnano l’identità fortemente architettonica, solo in apparenza “scomposta”, della costruzione del capo, senza penalizzarne la lievità, la sensazione forte di movimento che lo percorre anche grazie al gioco di plissettature a vista, né la morbida romantica anima cromatica, solare e rosata al contempo. Riflette questa aerea complessità dell’insieme il body: ridotto ai minimi termini per costruzione, è in verità assolutamente prezioso nella sua definizione decorativa. In questo completo, come in tutta la collezione, il leit motiv è costituito dall’accostamento singolare ed innovativo tra le tipologie più femminili ed aeree della seta e delle materie sostanzialmente estranee agli orizzonti dell’abbigliamento di lusso, in primis paglia di Vienna e rafia, che tuttavia riescono a creare impressioni sfarzo ed opulenza autentici. Secondo capo. il primo usato in sfilata è alla Galleria del Costume di Palazzo Pitti a Firenze ( donazione 2000)
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)

ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.acquisition: Soazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.materials: Taffetà di seta color paglia, organza di seta color paglia, rafia, tobolari di organza.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.description: Gonna lunga a terra di linea ampia completamente mossa da plissettatura e chiusura a zip laterale. Il punto vita è segnato da un “bouquet” composito, costituito da fiori, fiocchi, ciuffi e nastri nei diversi materiali.
ALTA-MODA/SS1989/advertising/slides/6325.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
